issues.comment: `&&` is used instead of `||` in the permission check, making `isTeamAdminOrOwner` true only for owners (since any owner passes `isTeamAdmin`, but only owners pass `isTeamOwner`). Team admins who are not owners will be incorrectly denied permission. The variable name `isTeamAdminOrOwner` clearly indicates `||` was intended. Should be: `(await isTeamAdmin(...)) || (await isTeamOwner(...))`.

`guests` (raw user input) is passed to `sendAddGuestsEmails` instead of `uniqueGuests` (the filtered list). In the email manager, this parameter is used to distinguish new guests (who get `AttendeeScheduledEmail`) from existing attendees (who get `AttendeeAddGuestsEmail`). If a user submits an email that's already an attendee, that email is in `guests` but filtered out of `uniqueGuests`, causing the existing attendee to incorrectly receive a full booking confirmation email instead of a "new guests added" notification. Should be `await sendAddGuestsEmails(evt, uniqueGuests)`.

The blacklisted emails are lowercased via `.toLowerCase()`, but the user-provided `guest` string is not lowercased before the `.includes()` check. A user can bypass the blacklist by using different casing, e.g., submitting `Blocked@Example.com` when `blocked@example.com` is blacklisted. Should be `!blacklistedGuestEmails.includes(guest.toLowerCase())`.
